title: Dilated Cardiomyopathy
chief_complaint: I can't breathe when I lie flat. I have to sleep sitting up with 3 pillows. My legs are swollen and I've gained 10 pounds in a week.

physical_exam.Cardiac: Tachycardia, S3 gallop, displaced PMI to 6th ICS anterior axillary line, holosystolic murmur at apex radiating to axilla (functional MR)
physical_exam.Extremities: Bilateral pitting edema to knees, cool extremities

hidden_data.echo: Severely dilated LV (LVEDD 6.8cm). Global hypokinesis. EF 20%. Severe functional mitral regurgitation. Moderate pulmonary hypertension (RVSP 55mmHg). No pericardial effusion.
hidden_data.ecg: Sinus tachycardia 108 bpm. LBBB (QRS 145ms). Poor R wave progression. LA enlargement.
hidden_data.cxr: Cardiomegaly (CTR 0.65). Pulmonary vascular congestion. Small bilateral pleural effusions.
hidden_data.cmp: Sodium 132, Potassium 3.8, Creatinine 1.3, BUN 30, normal LFTs
hidden_data.cath: Normal coronary arteries. No significant CAD.

correct_diagnosis: Dilated Cardiomyopathy (Non-Ischemic) with Acute Decompensated Heart Failure (Wet-Warm Profile)
key_learning_points: DCM = LV dilation + systolic dysfunction (EF <40%) without CAD, valvular disease, or congenital heart disease. Most common cause of heart failure in patients <50
Causes: Idiopathic (50%), Viral myocarditis, Alcohol, Genetic (30% familial), Pregnancy (peripartum), Tachycardia-induced, Autoimmune
Acute decompensated HF treatment: IV diuretics (Furosemide), vasodilators (Nitroglycerin if HTN), inotropes if shock (Dobutamine/Milrinone)
GDMT for chronic HFrEF: ARNI/ACEi/ARB + Beta-blocker + MRA + SGLT2i (4 pillars reduce mortality 50-60%)
LBBB with QRS >150ms + EF ≤35% = indication for CRT-D (reduces mortality 36% in MADIT-CRT trial)

patient_responses.onset: I was fine until about 3 months ago. I started getting short of breath walking. Now I can't even walk to the bathroom without gasping.
patient_responses.history: I used to drink heavily in my 20s and 30s but I've been sober for 10 years. No heart problems before this.
patient_responses.medications: None regularly. I took ibuprofen for back pain occasionally.
patient_responses.family: My uncle died of 'heart problems' at 45. My father has an enlarged heart.

feedback.correct_message: Excellent! You correctly diagnosed non-ischemic DCM causing acute decompensated HF. The echo shows severe LV dilation with EF 20%, normal coronaries on cath rule out ischemic etiology. GDMT including ARNI, beta-blocker, MRA, and SGLT2i is indicated, along with CRT-D evaluation.
feedback.attending_note: Admit for IV Furosemide 80mg IV BID, strict I/O, daily weights. Start GDMT: Sacubitril/Valsartan 24/26mg BID, Carvedilol 3.125mg BID (when euvolemic), Spironolactone 25mg daily, Dapagliflozin 10mg daily. Cardiac MRI to evaluate for myocarditis/scar. Refer for CRT-D (LBBB, QRS >150ms, EF ≤35%). Genetic testing for familial DCM. Family screening with echo.
